basic_info.about: The University of Rwanda (UR) is the largest higher education institution in Rwanda, formed in 2013 through the merger of seven previously independent public institutions. It operates as a multi-campus university with seven colleges across eight campuses, hosting four World Bank-funded African Centres of Excellence.
basic_info.summary: UR is Rwanda's premier public university, consistently ranked #1 nationally. It focuses on research-led education across diverse fields including STEM, medicine, and social sciences, with a strong emphasis on regional impact in Sub-Saharan Africa.

programs.total_programs_count: Over 138 postgraduate programs and numerous undergraduate programs offered across 6 colleges.

placements.graduate_outcomes_note: Placement data for 2026 is projected based on early graduation cycles and 2025 labor market trends showing a 2.5% decrease in youth unemployment.

placements.placement_highlights: The University of Rwanda maintains a strong employment rate for graduates, particularly in STEM fields which account for 64% of total graduates. Recent 2025/2026 labor surveys indicate a rising trend in service sector recruitment and high labor force participation (85.2%) for individuals with university qualifications.

fees.fees_note: Undergraduate fees at the University of Rwanda (UR) are typically structured by program type, with STEM courses costing roughly 900,000 RWF (~$670 USD) and non-STEM courses 600,000 RWF (~$445 USD) per year. Postgraduate costs vary significantly by module, averaging 2,500,000 RWF for a full Master's degree. PhD costs generally cover tuition and registration but exclude research-specific expenses.

scholarships_detail.name: UR Postgraduate Scholarship
scholarships_detail.eligibility: High-performing postgraduate applicants in priority research areas.

scholarships_detail.name: Government of Rwanda (HEC) Bursary
scholarships_detail.eligibility: Rwandan citizens with high national exam scores for undergraduate studies.

sources_verified: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at UR (mcfsp.ur.ac.rw)

infrastructure.details: Distributed across 9 campuses: Nyarugenge, Gikondo, Remera, Huye, Busogo, Nyagatare, Rukara, Rwamagana, and Rusizi.
University-wide ICT systems including data centers, networks, and client services managed across all colleges.
Includes various specialized centers like the African Center of Excellence in Internet of Things (ACEIoT) and Energy for Sustainable Development (ACE-ESD).

scholarships.name: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility: African students (Rwandan or refugees), under 28, academically talented but economically disadvantaged, focus on STEM and women.

scholarships.name: Government Study Loan (HEC)
scholarships.eligibility: Rwandan nationals admitted to UR, priority for STEM programs.

scholarships.name: Queen Elizabeth Commonwealth Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Citizens of Commonwealth countries (excluding Rwanda) for Master's degrees.

scholarships.name: UR-Sweden Program for Research Training
scholarships.eligibility: Staff and PhD aspirants for research training in collaboration with Swedish universities.
